title: rosa pisocarpa subsp. ahartii
intro: Rosa pisocarpa subsp. ahartii, also known as Ahart's wild rose, is a subspecies of wild rose that is native to the United States. The plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and inflammation, due to its content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Rosa pisocarpa subsp. ahartii include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Rosa pisocarpa subsp. ahartii can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
